Aggregated bias · 1-glance synthesis
Today's verdict NEUTRAL-CAUTIOUS
When the signals disagree · and which one to trust
Different gauges sometimes point opposite ways. That is normal. Here is each clash in plain words, with the side to lean on for now.
⚠ These two disagree: Crash Index **3/10** WARNING but still benign on realized price vs VIX Spike Tier **2/4** MEDIUM with **60%** spike probability, skew and backwardation both active
Spot tape is calm while the options market is paying for a vol expansion. Trust the VIX Spike Detector here: skew plus backwardation is a leading signal, the Crash Index is coincident.
⚠ These two disagree: Regime state **21** STABLE, no regime shift vs Divergence composite ELEVATED with **51.2%** of pairs diverging, **17** bearish vs **5** bullish
Headline regime is steady while cross-asset correlations are cracking under the hood. Trust the divergence read as the early warning, regime score lags by construction.
Today's tension
Options market pricing a vol expansion while price action stays calm and breadth broadens.
Bull
Macro regime reads **BROADENING** with breadth pairs like SPY/RSP correlation drifting from **+0.19** to **+0.88**, and global risk is bid with Kospi **+7.8%** and NDX **+4.1%** over five days. Regime state scores **21** in the STABLE zone, so the trend tape gives you no reason to sell.
Bear
VIX Spike Detector sits at tier **2/4** with a **60%** probability, skew active and futures in backwardation, while divergence composite is ELEVATED with **51.2%** of pairs diverging and **17** bearish versus **5** bullish. The options market is paying up for protection while correlations quietly snap.
The tell: Watch the SKEW/VIX pair: correlation drift from **-0.29** to **-0.82** is the cleanest read. If skew rolls over and that pair re-couples, the bear setup unwinds. If VIX backwardation deepens with HYG cracking, the T1 x HYG confluence flips live and you cut gross.
AI read · Citrini · today's macro tape

The tape is calm on the surface and stressed underneath, and that gap is the whole story today. Crash Index prints 3/10 in WARNING, regime state holds 21 STABLE, and the macro regime reads BROADENING at 30% confidence with global indices up across the board (Kospi +7.8%, STOXX50 +4.8%, NDX +4.1%). At the same time the VIX Spike Detector is at tier 2/4 MEDIUM with a 60% probability of a spike, skew active and the curve in backwardation, while 51.2% of cross-asset pairs are diverging with a net bearish bias of 12. Price says trend, options and correlations say tension.

For positioning you stay long the broadening trade but you stop pressing it. Contrarian score at 45 is ACCUMULATE on partial signal, not a full green light, and with zero confluence triggers active the tail risk is real but not imminent. Keep core exposure in the broadeners (equal-weight, Asia, Europe cyclicals), fund tail hedges out of the index calls that the 60% spike probability is mispricing relative to spot, and resist adding gross into the next leg up. This is a market where you protect the book, you don't lever it.

The risk that flips you defensive is a credit and small-cap break that lights up the dormant confluences. If T1 x HYG turns active, or if SKEW/VIX correlation stays pinned at -0.82 while regime score rolls below the STABLE band, cut equity gross and add duration. The clearest single tell is the divergence composite: a move from ELEVATED into EXTREME with bearish pairs above 20 is your exit on the bull case.

5-regime heuristic classifier · cross-asset
Macro Regime — where are we structurally
AI read · macro regime

Regime reads BROADENING at 30% confidence, which is the early-stage version of a rotation out of mega-cap leadership into the wider market. The 30% confidence is the caveat: it's a regime call you act on with smaller size, not a high-conviction trade.

The supporting evidence is the SPY/RSP correlation jump from +0.19 to +0.88 and global participation (Asia, Europe, US all green over five days). You position by trimming concentrated tech beta, adding equal-weight, cyclical, and ex-US exposure, and keeping convexity in vol products given the 60% VIX spike probability.

A broadening regime rewards diversification and punishes the crowded long, so you spread risk and stop chasing the top decile of YTD winners.

SPY above its 200-DMA with contained vol. This is the zone where long convexity compounds: leverage and call buying carry the lowest variance drag. Size up your highest-conviction directional calls here.
Gate from Leverage for the Long Run (Gayed/Bilello 2016): the durable edge is the 200-DMA × vol regime, the 3× compounding is marketing. Attention gauge for call sizing, not a trade trigger.

Dow Theory generalized · cross-asset divergences
Cross-asset divergence radar — Cross-asset divergence watch
AI read · divergences in plain English

The under-the-hood signal is loud: composite divergence is ELEVATED with 51.2% of 43 tracked pairs diverging, 17 bearish against only 5 bullish, and a Mother Jaws score of 100. A divergence here means two assets that usually move together have stopped doing so, and correlation drift is the size of that change over a rolling window.

The top jaws are telling: SKEW vs VIX drifted from -0.29 to -0.82 (tail hedging detaching from spot vol), LQD vs IEF went from +0.02 to +0.84 (investment grade credit moving with Treasuries, meaning rate-driven not risk-driven), and SPY vs RSP from +0.19 to +0.88 (the index and equal-weight finally moving together as breadth catches up). The picture is a broadening rally on the surface with funding and tail markets quietly repricing risk underneath, and that's the asymmetric setup you size for.

Cross-asset confluence · live status
Confluences — most proven alpha of the framework 0/3 ACTIVE
AI read · what the confluences mean now

Zero of the three tracked confluences are active, and that is the cleanest reason not to panic today. T1 x HYG (VIX backwardation with credit stress), T1 x B2 (VIX backwardation with small-cap drawdown), and T1 x JPY (VIX backwardation with yen safe-haven flow) are all quiet.

VIX backwardation is live on its own, but credit, small caps, and the yen are not confirming. You lean long broadeners with hedges on, because the absence of confluence is what keeps you in risk.

The moment HYG cracks or JPY catches a bid, the single-leg VIX warning becomes a multi-leg confluence and you cut. Until then, no confluence equals no forced action.

✅
T1 × HYG (VIX backwardation + Credit stress)
Volatility backwardation meeting credit stress — the highest-conviction pre-correction setup in the framework. Fires only when options fear and the bond market confirm each other.

Asia · Europe · US · Crypto 24/7 — lead-lag detection
Cross-Region Surveillance
Major indices from the 4 regions/asset classes. Asia trades ahead time-wise vs US — a Tokyo overnight crash often leaks into the SPX open. Crypto = 24h leading indicator of global risk appetite.
AI read · the cross-region tape

Asia is leading the world: Kospi +7.8% over five days makes Korea the global beta engine, while Hang Seng at -0.3% is the lone laggard and tells you China is not the source of the bid. Europe is constructive across the board with STOXX50 +4.8% leading and FTSE still positive at +2.5%, so the broadening trade is genuine and global.

In the US, NDX +4.1% beating SPX +2.1% means tech is still leading the US tape even as global breadth widens, which is the subtle tension: the US hasn't fully handed the baton to cyclicals yet. Crypto is risk-on with SOL +7.8% and BTC lagging at +1.2%, classic late-cycle risk appetite where the longer-tail asset outperforms the benchmark.

For today's US open, the read is: Asia and Europe set up a gap-up bid, but watch whether SPX can close the gap to NDX. If equal-weight and small caps lead intraday, the broadening regime confirms; if NDX extends alone, you're back in a narrow tape and the divergence warning gets louder.

Forward-looking indicators · Bonferroni-validated
Crash Index — 10 lead-signal radar 3/10 · WARNING — 3+ signals, active surveillance
AI read · volatility & crash, reconciled

Crash Index at 3/10 WARNING and VIX Spike tier 2/4 MEDIUM with 60% probability are not contradictory, they are describing two different clocks. The Crash Index is coincident, scoring realized price stress right now, and 3 signals mean the surface is fine.

The VIX Spike Detector is forward-looking: skew is active (tails are bid), futures are in backwardation (near-term vol priced above forward vol), so the options market is paying up for a vol expansion that hasn't shown up in spot yet. Adaptive isn't active, so this isn't a panic regime, it's a positioning regime where dealers and hedgers are reaching for protection while the index drifts.

You trust the VIX signal as the early warning and use the calm Crash print as your window to hedge cheap, not as confirmation everything is fine.

Plain-English read · the Crash Index
Think of this as a smoke alarm, not a market forecast. Each of the 10 rows is a signal that led real crashes, checked over 15 years of S&P 500 history (Bonferroni-validated, so it survived a strict false-positive test). In a calm market it sits near zero by design — a low number is the alarm working, not a broken gauge. It only earns your attention when 3 or more rows light up together. Today it reads 3/10 — a few early signals are stirring; watch whether more line up.

3/10

Score 0-2 CALM · 3-4 WATCH · 5-6 WARNING · 7-8 ALARM · 9-10 EXTREME (rare crash imminent). Lead times: structural 30-90d · stress 5-30d · triggers 1-10d. Four Telegram alerts available depending on escalation.
How to read
Each row = an independent signal Bonferroni-validated over 15 years of SPY. The structural (DXY, SKEW, MOVE) signal a regime change over 30-90 days. The stress (HYG/IEF, VIX3M/VIX, STLFSI4) ring at 5-30 days. The triggers (SPY -2% in 5d, VIX +30% in 3d) are short-term breakouts. For a tactical decision: 3+ signals simultaneously aligned = real probability of stress, never on isolated signal (framework rule).

vs baseline 37% · backtested cycle 2023-2026
Action — sizing tied to tier
Buy hedges B-tier (1-2% book) — VIX calls 30-60 DTE strike 25-28 OR SPY puts 60-90 DTE Δ0.20-0.30
How to read
4 tiers (BROAD / MEDIUM / HIGH / EXTREME) based on the confluence of 3 Validated components: SKEW > 140 (tail-hedge demand), VIX3M/VIX > 1.05 (vol curve inverted), VIX < 16 + ratio > 1.15 (adaptive proxy). Each tier indicates a historical probability of VIX +50% at 60d (cycle backtest 2023-2026, vs baseline 37%). Strict sizing: tier 2 = 1-2% book hedges, tier 3 = 3-5% long-vol, tier 4 = 5-10% MAX (rare confluence).

Aggregated bias · 53-ratio composite
Regime synthesis — RISK-ON / OFF / STRESS / TOPPING
MIXED — competing signals No clean composite per Kerberos. Wait for alignment.
RISK-ON aligned (3)
• Small caps leading
• Copper > Gold
• Industrials > Utilities
RISK-OFF aligned (1)
• ETH dumping vs BTC
STRESS aligned (1)
• Dollar wrecking ball
TOPPING aligned (0)
none
How to read
Aggregation of all panel signals across the 4 cross-asset buckets. RISK-ON = risk appetite (cyclical, equity, EM). RISK-OFF = flight to defensives (TLT, gold, JPY). STRESS = liquidity tension (HYG, MOVE, STLFSI). TOPPING = end-of-cycle signals (concentration, breadth narrowing, defensives leading). Doctrine: a single signal = noise. 3+ aligned in one bucket = real confirmed regime.

EV% = win_rate × (target−entry) − (1−win_rate) × (entry−stop). Tier = T1 highest conviction. 52w% = % of 52-week high. Entry/stop/target levels live on the Trade Desk →
How to read
Heat ranking: composite of RS (relative strength vs SPY), Momentum (price action 20-60d), Breadth (% stocks above SMA), Options flow, and Attention. Use top-5 as rotation focus.
Emerging industries: high velocity = screener scores accelerating this week vs prior baseline — potential early rotation signal.
Competitor themes: token frequency × recency across top finance Substacks — useful narrative context, not a trading signal.
Best R/R: only entries with an active screener leg (PULLBACK / BOTTOM / SMART_MONEY_SURGE etc.). EV% = probabilistic edge per trade. Cross-check with weekly chart before sizing.

Reading the table — Z-Score: number of standard deviations vs rolling 100d mean. |Z| > 2 = Kerberos entry threshold, |Z| > 2.5 = extreme (best R/R on mean-reversion). %B: position within Bollinger Bands (1.0 = upper band, 0 = lower band).

Aggregate reading — the mean of Z-scores gives a global regime indicator. Positive → cross-asset overbought market (Risk-On, watch reversal). Negative → oversold (Risk-Off, possible bottoming).
How to read
Persist = number of consecutive days above |Z| > 2.0 (Kerberos threshold). Persist ≥ 3d = validated signal (the divergence has become a regime, not a spike). Persist 1-2d = to watch. Persist 0d = dead signal. Sort on this column to find established regimes worth trading.
Glossary — 18 terms to understand the dashboard
Z-Score
Number of standard deviations vs the historical mean (rolling 100 days). Z=0 = normal level · |Z|>2 = stress · |Z|>2.5 = extreme.
Bollinger Bands
Bands drawn at ±2.2σ around a 100-day moving average. When the price exits the bands = statistically extreme signal.
Backwardation
Inverted curve: immediate fear exceeds far fear (e.g. VIX > VIX3M). Short-term panic signal.
Contango
Normal situation where far fear exceeds immediate fear. Extreme contango = complacency.
Half-life
Number of days required for a gap to the mean to halve. Measures the speed of mean-reversion.
Cointegration
Two assets that move together in the long term despite short-term fluctuations. Statistical basis of pair-trading.
Mean-reversion
Strategy betting that a gap to the mean will close. Inverse of momentum (which bets on continuation).
Breadth
Width of participation in the rally. Good breadth = many stocks rising. Bad breadth = only a few mega-caps pull the index.
Hedge ratio
Quantity of asset B to short to neutralize 1 long unit of asset A. Calculated via OLS regression on historical returns.
Lead-time
Delay between a signal's appearance and the event it predicts. Structural = 30-90d · stress = 5-30d · triggers = 1-10d.
Bonferroni
Statistical correction for multiple testing. When testing 100 signals, some seem valid by chance. Bonferroni divides the significance threshold by the number of tests to eliminate these false positives.
MCPT (Monte Carlo Permutation Test)
Robustness test: data is randomly shuffled 5000-10000 times to see if the signal appears by chance. If p < 0.05, the signal is probably real.
Walk-forward
Test where the model is trained on the first years (e.g. 2010-2020) and tested on the following years (2020-2025). Avoids in-sample overfitting.
Tail-hedge
Hedge against an extreme crash (-20% or more). Bought via deeply out-of-the-money puts. SKEW measures institutional demand for this type of insurance.
Mega-caps
The 7-10 largest market caps in the SPX (AAPL, MSFT, NVDA, GOOGL, AMZN, META, TSLA). Weigh ~35% of the index.
Cap-weighted vs Equal-weighted
SPY = weighted by capitalization (mega-caps dominate). RSP = each stock weighs 0.2%. Comparing both reveals concentration.
Risk parity
Portfolio strategy that balances risk (not capital) across asset classes. Stressed when stocks/bonds correlations turn positive (inflation regime).
framework rule
Fundamental principle: a solo signal = noise, 3+ aligned signals = real edge. All panel signals are Bonferroni-validated over 15 years of SPY.
